---
| _The reviews are in on Governor Eric Holcomb's State of the State Address...
_**and it's a hit!**__
---
| | **FROM INDIANA'S LEADERS:**
---
| _In case you missed it, you can watch the full speech[HERE NOW](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=55b3d165f4&e=1acd9bb3d6), and then check out the great reviews it’s getting from Hoosiers across the state:_
---
| "Governor Eric Holcomb demonstrated strong and consistent leadership in his third State of the State Address. He proved yet again that he is armed and ready to tackle the issues facing Indiana with ideas that will deliver measurable and positive results for all Hoosiers. Through his series of Next Level initiatives, Governor Holcomb is providing Hoosiers with innovative solutions to our state’s biggest challenges – from teacher pay to workforce development, and from infrastructure funding to attacking the drug epidemic. And, even as we address these issues with new dollars and fresh ideas, Governor Holcomb is committed to preserving Indiana’s status as the fiscal envy of the nation by protecting our AAA credit rating and maintaining reserves at an impressive 11 percent...There’s a reason why Governor Holcomb’s approval rating stands at 65 percent. He is delivering on behalf of Hoosiers in every corner of the state and tonight he did it again." **-Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer**

_
"Our governor Eric Holcomb continues to build upon the successes the legislature and previous administrations have made. His plans to increase teacher pay and develop a skilled workforce will pay dividends for future generations of Hoosiers. It is imperative we are keeping on the path of taking Indiana and those who call it home to the Next Level, and I look forward to working with our governor to meet our goals." **-Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**

“Governor Holcomb set the right tone with his bold vision for our state’s future, and we will continue working with him to take our shared priorities across the finish line this session. To elevate our state’s most important profession for the future, we appreciate the governor’s commitment to taking meaningful and substantive steps to put more dollars into teachers’ pockets. While teacher salary decisions are made entirely at the local level, his call for the state to use reserves to pay off a pension liability that schools currently pay should help schools pass on about $140 million in savings over the next two years directly to teachers. This action coupled with several of our legislative priorities this session, are part of a short- and long-term goal to keep hardworking teachers in Indiana’s classrooms.” **-Speaker Brian Bosma**

__
“In his 2019 State of the State address, Gov. Holcomb outlined the many successes our state has achieved and the challenges before us. Indiana’s economy is strong, and as we continue to attract businesses to our state, it is critical that we continue to improve job-training opportunities for Hoosiers across Indiana. We must also continue to support our teachers and schools, look for ways to fight the drug epidemic and expand addiction-treatment options, and protect our most vulnerable youth.” **-Senate President Pro Tem Rod Bray**

__
"We're encouraged - as many Hoosiers are - by the continued forward-looking approach taken by Gov. Holcomb and his administration. Investments to grow and attract Indiana's current and future workers are required to allow our state to achieve at the highest levels..."In regard to the bias crimes issue, we are pleased to see the Governor continue to emphasize its importance. It is imperative this year to find a way to remove Indiana from the short list of states without a bias crimes law." **\- Kevin Brinegar, Indiana Chamber of Commerce President and CEO**

__
“Finding qualified workers in the current tight job market is now the top concern of small businesses. Gov. Holcomb’s desire to expand workforce training and allow more Hoosiers to gain the high-level skills required in today’s workforce is admirable. By encouraging career education in secondary schools and offering training to inmates, the number of qualified workers should increase... We applaud the Governor for addressing this workforce problem and hope lawmakers will realize its importance.” **\- Barbara Quandt Underwood, National Federation of Independent Business Indiana State Director**

name: TAKE ACTION (via Lipinski)
subject: let’s get this commonsense reform passed

body:
There are no good reasons for Congress to not pass universal background checks on ALL gun sales in America.
We have legislation to see it done. Dan co-sponsored it.
We need you to urge Congress to pass it. Add your name now → [http://petitions.signforgood.com/SupportFederalBackgroundChecks/?code=lipinski&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fdl-backgroundchecks-ja-up%3Famount%3D10.00%26recurring%3Dtrue]
Begin forwarded message: http://www.lipinskiforcongress.com/ [http://www.lipinskiforcongress.com/]
Peter,
It’s time to make universal background checks on all gun sales a reality in this country.
An overwhelming majority of Americans support this policy, but time and time again Congress has failed to get it done. This commonsense measure can and will save lives.
That’s why I cosponsored the most recent House bill to make universal background checks the law of the land. Peter, will you help me see it through?
I’m asking all of my supports to click here and urge Congress to pass this legislation now. We MUST show that the public wholeheartedly supports this. [https://petitions.signforgood.com/SupportFederalBackgroundChecks/?code=lipinski&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fdl-backgroundchecks-ja-up%3Famount%3D10.00%26recurring%3Dtrue]
ADD YOUR NAME [https://petitions.signforgood.com/SupportFederalBackgroundChecks/?code=lipinski&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fdl-backgroundchecks-ja-up%3Famount%3D10.00%26recurring%3Dtrue]
There is no reason that anyone with a history of domestic violence or a criminal past should be able to legally obtain deadly weapons.
When background checks are required, significant drops in homicide and suicide rates follow.
I’ve always made it a priority to work across the aisle on pragmatic solutions to our country’s biggest problems, and that’s why this legislation is so incredibly important to me.
Requiring background checks on ALL gun sales in America is just plain common sense.
Please click here to stand with me and urge Congress to enact universal background checks now → [https://petitions.signforgood.com/SupportFederalBackgroundChecks/?code=lipinski&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fdl-backgroundchecks-ja-up%3Famount%3D10.00%26recurring%3Dtrue]
Thank you.
Dan

Donald Trump’s federal government shutdown is now in its 28th day, making it the longest impasse in U.S. history.

By the end of this month, Food and Nutrition Assistance funding could run dry for over 900,000 low-income Tennesseans.

More than 25,000 civilian federal employees live in Tennessee and are not getting paid.

By next week, the budget impasse will have costed our economy $6 billion.

Right now, Senate Majority Leader Mitch McConnell is playing politics by blocking a vote on a bill -- to reopen the government, and pay our federal workers -- that he and Senate Republicans overwhelmingly supported just a month ago.

With a chance to stand up for her constituents by urging Leader Mitch McConnell to call a vote on the budget plan, Marsha Blackburn continues to put party loyalty before the well-being of Tennesseans and our country.

Enough is enough -- Will you pitch $25, $50, or $100 to help hold Republicans like Marsha Blackburn accountable? https://act.myngp.com/Forms/6954468427655547904?am=&

Mary

--

Mary Mancini

Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

subject: SCOTUS Watch: Two Prayer Alerts
body:
| | Critical Updates from the current session of the Supreme Court
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66ie-EuEwprJECnklQBd9pnr0rgh7VwmTCIoO8KZdC1FONfJKGwxf9I3aIVUfopA8_Vw&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190118com12&n=1)
| The Supreme Court of the United States (SCOTUS) will hear oral arguments for a total of [11 cases](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=WqnSJN-UqkPJD0rrvzw7zpfxwH6pCVCaFEa30IBMq_en40Jb_9S1JInHF1Q6NwTorTfic_mTVgAAUFjxkodl7NdTSCI-odFrmggSMT4mDt26npWDfIu5rYVDAXyFFsr03x7Wu_IpGhBP9Eo15__grA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190118com12&n=2) this month. It’s worth noting Ginsburg is still recovering from a surgery she had on December 21st and has been participating in the court’s consideration of cases from home through briefs and transcripts of the arguments. **PRAYER ALERT** This is the first time in 25 years that Justice Ginsburg (age 85) has not been on the bench to hear a Supreme Court Case. Pray for Justice Ginsburg to recover well from the surgery and for her to have wisdom as she works from home on our nation’s most important cases. As Justice Ginsburg continues to struggle with health challenges, pray she comes to know Jesus Christ as her personal Lord and Savior. Also, please be praying now for the [Bladensburg Memorial Cross Case](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=9AOqGvHDl3ctglrEUlhy-YxVKicwlzh-U9h70PyyVCd49nScgr3lFQLBbOYw37iS&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190118com12&n=3). This is an extremely important case for the preservation of religious liberty in our nation. SCOTUS will hear oral arguments for this case on February 27, 2019. We will be providing you with more information on this case next month. **SUPREME COURT CASE UPDATE for JANUARY** Below are three cases SCOTUS recently heard oral arguments for, that are worth highlighting. **Case 1: Tennessee Wine & Spirits Retailers Association v. Byrd** In order to obtain a retail license to sell alcohol in Tennessee, state law requires the individual to live in the state a minimum of two years. This law is now in question because a federal appeals court ruled that it violates the Constitution because it discriminates against out-of-state residents. On the other side, the Trade Association of liquor retailers believes the 21st Amendment provides states with extensive authority to regulate alcohol how they see fit. **_The central question in this case_ :** Does the Twenty-first Amendment empower States, consistent with the dormant Commerce Clause, to regulate liquor sales by granting licenses only to individuals or entities that have resided in-state for a specified time? Oral arguments for this case were heard on January 16, 2019. **Case 2: Azar v. Allina Health Services** The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) used a new process to determine Medicare reimbursement rates. They did so because of changes under President Obama’s administration to the reimbursements hospitals receive for treating a disproportionate number of low-income patients. The lawsuit is regarding the Department of Health and Human Services implementation of its rule. The hospitals argue that HHS should have required notice and a comment period before requiring Medicare’s contract administrators to implement the change. What’s at stake is an estimated $3 to $4 billion in reimbursements for hospitals between the time period of 2005 and 2013. Justice Brett Kavanaugh has recused himself from this case because he wrote the lower court opinion when he was serving as a judge on the District of Columbia Circuit Court. You can read Justice Kavanaugh’s opinion on the piece [here](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=zaqeHqBFdeKljM4DvcBB9Dgkv6QA7CVa7_-t0D0-Or6GqFbveCzZScDzCrognxB37EH7ePvzffVi7M7Va2m_k1fFUkMw_gF5366kN5F3NcE&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190118com12&n=4). **_The central question in this case_ :** [Was HHS required to conduct notice-and-comment rulemaking before changing the Medicare reimbursement formula?](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hMo-caygu3bh-xw0ick3kq095080K82Ld_cUY9yZoaw-tW7SUgX_1h4OkJRMTnSGNg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190118com12&n=5) Oral arguments for this case were heard on January 15, 2019. **Case 3: Knick v. Township of Scott, Pennsylvania** The court first heard arguments for the Knick v. Township of Scott case in October but Justice Kavanaugh was not yet on the bench. The Township of Scott, in Pennsylvania, has an ordinance requiring all cemeteries on public or private land to be accessible to the general public during the day. Rose Mary Knick owns 90 acres of property and law enforcement agents claim to have found stones on her property that they believe to be grave markers. Ms. Knick argues there is no existence of a cemetery on her property. She filed suit claiming a Fifth Amendment violation. However, the plaintiff must exhaust all state court remedies first before taking it to the federal level. A 1985 Supreme Court precedent, Williamson County Regional Planning Commission v. Hamilton Bank, requires that plaintiffs must go through the state’s compensation procedure first to obtain just compensation before taking their case to a federal court. **_The central question in this case_ : **[Should SCOTUS overturn the exhaustion requirement established in Williamson County Regional Planning Commission v. Hamilton Bank?](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=RDRHk6ni7OvEv8Bu9zj6hAM7-qJiPEUfL8R7f6Lx54l941dsxnh29xkL0dj-HnnQ3UrdX_IgJVC0wbkWB2vZ6pmOy5ULF2gqb8tUJr85UgY&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e190118com12&n=6) Oral arguments for this case were heard on January 16, 2019. Please continue to pray for the SCOTUS justices as they determine their decisions on the 11 cases they heard this month. Pray for them to have God’s wisdom to issue decisions that uphold the Constitution, value the states’ rights, and protect the rights of all Americans.

| **_Leaders in Place_** This week, I’ve been thinking a lot about our future – both as a party and as a state. And I’ve come to the conclusion that while Indiana may be in a great position today, our state’s future is even brighter. And that’s because of our forward-thinking leaders in place today, and the leaders who are coming up through the ranks right now.

I’ll start with **Governor Eric Holcomb**. There’s a reason why Hoosiers have him at an 65% approval rating. He is tackling our state’s biggest issues head-on, and he’s doing it by offering bold leadership and real solutions. Whether it’s innovative ways to fund infrastructure _(Indiana’s the only state with a fully-funded infrastructure plan of its kind_), skill-up Hoosiers (_with popular grants for earning industry credentials or gaining on-the-job training_) or attack the drug epidemic, **Governor Holcomb** is working 24-7-365 to take Indiana – as he said in his [State of the State this week](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f596befbd8&e=1acd9bb3d6) – to the “next level.”

But leadership doesn’t just stop at the top. This week **Senate President Pro Tem Rodric Bray** accepted a big honor (more on that below), and last year’s all-female statewide team of **Secretary of State Connie Lawson** , **Auditor Tera Klutz** and **Treasurer Kelly Mitchell** were sworn-in for another term in office. And then, of course, there’s our Republican mayors – the leaders on the front lines of making our Hoosier communities top places to live, work, and play.

Then there’s also the next generation of leaders...who I had a chance to speak with on Monday night at the Indiana University College Republicans meeting. These students are already actively engaged in our party, and are a key part of our boots-on-the-ground operation talking to voters ahead of elections. They bring energy, drive, and fresh ideas to our party and to our state.

We’ve got the team in place. And led by **Governor Holcomb** , this team of leaders will continue to take Indiana to the Next Level.

-Kyle Hupfer

**PHOTO:** _Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer drove down to Bloomington on Monday to speak to the IU College Republicans. He talked about our record 2018 – from fundraising to the ballot box and beyond – and the great range of opportunities available today for young Hoosiers looking to get involved in public service in Indiana!_
---
|

| | | **GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b2279e5868&e=1acd9bb3d6)

| **_Next Level State of the State Address_** **Governor Eric Holcomb **delivered a strong State of the State address this week! In case you missed it, [you can watch it HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0042db4c69&e=1acd9bb3d6), and you’ll hear about the bold leadership he’s bringing to tackle our state’s biggest issues. This includes two issues that made news this week – efforts to boost teacher pay and pass a bias crimes law. _**When it comes to boosting teacher pay...**_ The goal is to make salaries more competitive when compared to neighboring states, allowing Indiana to attract and retain the best teachers. As **Governor Holcomb** said, “A strong economy depends on a world-class workforce. That workforce depends on a great education. A great education depends on great teachers.” The governor’s plan is to free up money that schools are currently spending on teacher pensions, directing limited surplus dollars of $140 million over the next two years to pay off a pension liability. He’s suggesting that local school districts allocate 100% of these savings to boosting teacher pay. _**With bias crimes legislation...**_ **Governor Holcomb** supports legislation that would strengthen Indiana law by ensuring judges can sentence more severely when a crime targets a group – even if there may only be one actual victim. This law does not criminalize or police thought to impact freedom of speech – it only comes into play when a _crime_ has been committed and recognizes that certain crimes have a deeper impact on a larger group – and not just one victim. This would take Indiana off the list of five states that lack a bias crimes law, and open Indiana up to more economic opportunities with companies looking for a place to locate, grow and create jobs.

| **_Senate President Pro Tem Bray Picked As A National Emerging Leader_** Congratulations to **Indiana Senate President Pro Temp Rodric Bray**! This week, he was selected as one of just 21 state legislators from across the country to be a part of GOPAC’s Emerging Leaders program. A nationally-recognized training initiative, the year-long program coaches and develops state legislators on how to be even more effective legislative leaders.

**Senator Bray** is already doing great work leading the Indiana Senate, and this honor is well deserved! Congratulations, **Senator Bray**!

This all-female statewide team was re-elected last year, and this week they were each ceremonially sworn in for another term in office serving Hoosiers.

In case you missed it, check out the full video of their swearing-in ceremony at the Statehouse [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d1fcea3b75&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

clean_body: This week, I’ve been thinking a lot about our future – both as a party and as a state. And I’ve come to the conclusion that while Indiana may be... | | | | | | http:// | Leaders in Place This week, I’ve been thinking a lot about our future – both as a party and as a state. And I’ve come to the conclusion that while Indiana may be in a great position today, our state’s future is even brighter. And that’s because of our forward-thinking leaders in place today, and the leaders who are coming up through the ranks right now. I’ll start with Governor Eric Holcomb. There’s a reason why Hoosiers have him at an 65% approval rating. He is tackling our state’s biggest issues head-on, and he’s doing it by offering bold leadership and real solutions. Whether it’s innovative ways to fund infrastructure (Indiana’s the only state with a fully-funded infrastructure plan of its kind), skill-up Hoosiers (with popular grants for earning industry credentials or gaining on-the-job training) or attack the drug epidemic, Governor Holcomb is working 24-7-365 to take Indiana – as he said in his – to the “next level.” But leadership doesn’t just stop at the top. This week Senate President Pro Tem Rodric Bray accepted a big honor (more on that below), and last year’s all-female statewide team of Secretary of State Connie Lawson , Auditor Tera Klutz and Treasurer Kelly Mitchell were sworn-in for another term in office. And then, of course, there’s our Republican mayors – the leaders on the front lines of making our Hoosier communities top places to live, work, and play. Then there’s also the next generation of leaders...who I had a chance to speak with on Monday night at the Indiana University College Republicans meeting. These students are already actively engaged in our party, and are a key part of our boots-on-the-ground operation talking to voters ahead of elections. They bring energy, drive, and fresh ideas to our party and to our state. We’ve got the team in place. And led by Governor Holcomb , this team of leaders will continue to take Indiana to the Next Level. -Kyle Hupfer PHOTO: Indiana GOP Chairman Kyle Hupfer drove down to Bloomington on Monday to speak to the IU College Republicans. He talked about our record 2018 – from fundraising to the ballot box and beyond – and the great range of opportunities available today for young Hoosiers looking to get involved in public service in Indiana! | | | | GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS | | Next Level State of the State Address Governor Eric Holcomb delivered a strong State of the State address this week! In case you missed it, , and you’ll hear about the bold leadership he’s bringing to tackle our state’s biggest issues. This includes two issues that made news this week – efforts to boost teacher pay and pass a bias crimes law. When it comes to boosting teacher pay... The goal is to make salaries more competitive when compared to neighboring states, allowing Indiana to attract and retain the best teachers. As Governor Holcomb said, “A strong economy depends on a world-class workforce. That workforce depends on a great education. A great education depends on great teachers.” The governor’s plan is to free up money that schools are currently spending on teacher pensions, directing limited surplus dollars of $140 million over the next two years to pay off a pension liability. He’s suggesting that local school districts allocate 100% of these savings to boosting teacher pay. With bias crimes legislation... Governor Holcomb supports legislation that would strengthen Indiana law by ensuring judges can sentence more severely when a crime targets a group – even if there may only be one actual victim. This law does not criminalize or police thought to impact freedom of speech – it only comes into play when a crime has been committed and recognizes that certain crimes have a deeper impact on a larger group – and not just one victim. This would take Indiana off the list of five states that lack a bias crimes law, and open Indiana up to more economic opportunities with companies looking for a place to locate, grow and create jobs. | | http:// | Senate President Pro Tem Bray Picked As A National Emerging Leader Congratulations to Indiana Senate President Pro Temp Rodric Bray! This week, he was selected as one of just 21 state legislators from across the country to be a part of GOPAC’s Emerging Leaders program. A nationally-recognized training initiative, the year-long program coaches and develops state legislators on how to be even more effective legislative leaders. Senator Bray is already doing great work leading the Indiana Senate, and this honor is well deserved! Congratulations, Senator Bray! | | | | Statewide Team Sworn In for New Term Congratulations to Secretary of State Connie Lawson , Auditor Tera Klutz and Treasurer Kelly Mitchell! This all-female statewide team was re-elected last year, and this week they were each ceremonially sworn in for another term in office serving Hoosiers. In case you missed it, check out the full video of their swearing-in ceremony at the Statehouse ! | | | Lugar Series Next Generation Do you know a young woman between the ages of 13-18 interested in learning more about government and politics? The Lugar Series is hosting another Next Generation Training Day this March to help more talented young women get involved! name: J.D. Scholten
subject: An update from J.D. Scholten
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten4Iowa Campaign Committee
body:
Peter,
I know it's been a while since you have heard from me. Rest assured, I have been busy.
Iowa's 4th District has been at the center of attention, yet again, thanks to our Representative choosing to embarrass us with his offensive rhetoric that betrays our American ideals. Despite Steve King's well-known record of racism and divisiveness over his 16 years in Congress, it took all of YOU and YOUR support for my supposedly "long-shot" campaign before Republicans in Iowa and Washington D.C. saw fit to also condemn him. Only now is he suffering the consequences of his actions because we showed the nation he was vulnerable and that many Iowans do NOT share his values.
I can't thank you enough for your belief in our campaign and know that I am considering all options as I think through how best I can serve Iowa. Please stay tuned for an announcement soon about what’s next.
I am very proud of the attention our campaign generated on how Democrats can regain the hearts of rural voters. Here are some recent press clips in case you missed them:
**[My Op-Ed in USA Today on why Steve King keeps winning ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4314969/34206846/-407852258?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0N2MwZTA1MC03YzFiLWU5MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lahjO-xvu4JzABDtXuFnBnsCj1J1RCrJmR3oUYSXvDo=)**

**[A great Washington Monthly feature on our campaign showing how Democrats can regain the rural vote](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4314972/34206849/-507930291?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0N2MwZTA1MC03YzFiLWU5MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lahjO-xvu4JzABDtXuFnBnsCj1J1RCrJmR3oUYSXvDo=)**
**[A nice feature and update on Sioux City Sue](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4314973/34206850/-1450742264?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0N2MwZTA1MC03YzFiLWU5MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lahjO-xvu4JzABDtXuFnBnsCj1J1RCrJmR3oUYSXvDo=)**
Two podcasts I did, **[one with Joe Gomez on his "American Grit" show](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4314974/34206851/-655090863?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0N2MwZTA1MC03YzFiLWU5MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lahjO-xvu4JzABDtXuFnBnsCj1J1RCrJmR3oUYSXvDo=&utm_medium=widget&utm_source=user%3A10976859&utm_term=episode_title)** and **[another for the BossCast where I talk Bruce Springsteen and the campaign](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/4314975/34206852/-1332331643?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDEvMS82MTU4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0N2MwZTA1MC03YzFiLWU5MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4NzgzOTFlZmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lahjO-xvu4JzABDtXuFnBnsCj1J1RCrJmR3oUYSXvDo=)**

Still Standing Tall for All,
J.D. Scholten

**I am unwaveringly pro-life.** It’s just who I am.

As a father of four and a grandfather of six, I know that every life is a gift from God.

Today, pro-life advocates from across the country, including hundreds of Hoosiers, marched on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. to stand up for our shared pro-life values.

**[If you stand for life like I do, SHARE our post on Facebook.](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=d97f38e3c3bb4798a4de69db71f5b6c8&_e=JyA3nMGqQgxqD8my5viKwr1meY89AnTh1A4Ti1YRn6wzCxkKO6dIWZ0FLUdPDnWCvtcUuH39SVM7Q6yjs3Hf8Y7D8wtzvsaClhFd1KwO29YNn3SKQYqbTK6K4dpNXbihVZuG3pg3AbxBbuuVw4mwXleDS3sKl81Vw_dVLLr79LgawIM69nGY5EGVATT-COL0jP2Lead5AhV0_CH8Jz-9uh-B06Vqbm0X1JjIFF-hLW_7VhSOtncuI35oYIKz1LkVLdas4nEEuFUeqCm4WLNVnqrZOBy4iPIxzyhAe9KBE_WT8EpHA6K-A8U-29a91899WCSmZFGOusjx6D6NEYAf3XRXQIHXEXCMcck4oaChuWsqURw1fNME4W5845NlSYMbjXEcvQto2AAJztHbcAFJ6-PA0CvR_lEGax602N1PJb_HGutu9_FDZuJhv4_e2wxD)**

I’m proud of those who march and stand up for their beliefs - for believing that every single human life, including the unborn, is deserving of protection and love.

I want to extend a special thank you and wish safe travels to all of the Hoosiers who traveled to Washington D.C. to stand up for life, and I encourage all who **[share our values to share our post on Facebook to spread the message: We stand for life.](http://links.gregpenceforcongress.com/u/click?_t=c56a42391aaa4fc29673628e10310dda&_m=d97f38e3c3bb4798a4de69db71f5b6c8&_e=JyA3nMGqQgxqD8my5viKwr1meY89AnTh1A4Ti1YRn6wzCxkKO6dIWZ0FLUdPDnWCvtcUuH39SVM7Q6yjs3Hf8Y7D8wtzvsaClhFd1KwO29YNn3SKQYqbTK6K4dpNXbihVZuG3pg3AbxBbuuVw4mwXleDS3sKl81Vw_dVLLr79LgawIM69nGY5EGVATT-COL0jP2Lead5AhV0_CH8Jz-9uh-B06Vqbm0X1JjIFF-hLW9h9ADR976Dyma6Ho4BJ6uWfNHB0PdawNoYCpbW8tqWm_RAI2r3H8nHl1UcdK9DJpdMFvhDZRCV30K7PyFa6lBaHIkuEmFzw5vRdN29ReLEn_xxJBk2qHEcbDpyhrCnt7wa0eKge17lFT4made4LbvrNud5fBTN97nEV0r28G4qjuLmGfWmNIPSPUT2iAgkrac1-Wc-7_tk8dTgH9oIt8sg)**

God Bless,
Greg Pence
